title: Object 69
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Object 69: '''Paralysis Glove'''}} {{Object Class|utility=4|risk=4|properties=Paralysis to an organism’s limb or part}} ==Description== '''Object 69''', commonly referred to as the '''Paralysis Glove''', is a rare object found across many different levels. The object has the appearance of a glove, with its exterior covered with a fur reminiscent of an animal, its texture is soft yet unnervingly dense, though it is unknown which animal this fur belongs to, as DNA analysis has been unsuccessful. The fur possesses the ability to paralyze a limb or any part of a living organism. Upon contact with the fur, an organism’s nerve signals in the affected area are halted, as it was found that the signals sent by the nerves were ignored by the body part. Although prolonged exposure can extend the effect, it typically lasts anywhere from 30 minutes to 2 hours. {{Blurimage|file=Goober glove.jpg|hovertext=Descent|caption=Digital representation of a '''Paralysis Glove'''.}} The object can be found inside of boxes, on top of tables, and on the ground. It is primarily encountered in indoor levels, such as [[Level 4]], [[Level 5]], [[Level 20]], [[Level 21]] etc. == Effects and Side Effects== Upon contact, the affected area quickly becomes numb, often accompanied by a tingling sensation before paralysis sets in. Users have reported experiencing slight recovery pain once the paralysis wears off. The duration of paralysis is not entirely consistent. In most cases, paralysis lasts between 30 minutes and 2 hours; however, the exact length appears to depend on factors such as the size of the contact area, prolonged exposure, and possibly the strength or density of the organism’s nervous system. Prolonged or repeated use of the glove on the same area of the body has not been thoroughly tested, but preliminary observations suggest the potential for nerve damage with repeated exposure. On entities, frequent application has shown to reduce mobility over time, possibly as a result of accumulated nerve trauma. This makes the object capable of managing or “wearing down” entities or threats in long-term engagements. There are various methods to treat or potentially reverse the paralysis on a limb or part. The most common approach is waiting for it to resolve on its own. Another method is using medicine to treat the paralysis, this could be medicine from the [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]], like pain relievers and muscle spasms. An alternative medicine is almond water, pouring it to the paralyzed area doesn’t completely reverse it, as it will only shorten the duration of the paralysis or relieve pain. ==Usage== '''Paralysis Glove''' can be used to paralyze humans or entities such as [[Entity 8|Hounds]], [[Entity 10|Skin Stealers]], [[Entity 5|Clumps]], etc. Some of the simplest methods of paralyzing the entities include slapping them in the face, punching them, or hurling the glove towards them. However, these methods may not always be effective as it’s only a temporary solution. Additionally, the object has shown reduced effectiveness on larger entities or those with less centralized nervous systems, meaning some targets may have natural resistance. However, the [[M.E.G.|M.E.G]] research team was successful in creating a weapon cable of permanently paralyzing threats. ===Paralysis Bullets=== Object 69-A, referred to as '''Paralysis Bullets''', are artificial objects created by [[M.E.G.|M.E.G]] to replicate and enhance the '''Paralysis Glove’s''' effects for tactical use. Each bullet contains a small but potent piece of fur embedded within a high-density metal casing, with a sharp metal tip. They are designed to penetrate the skin and are primarily shot at the brain, as they are more effective against critical areas where nervous control is centralized, with the intent to cause permanent paralysis to the victim. Currently, their use is restricted to emergencies and missions only. [[M.E.G]] personnel must undergo specialized training to handle these bullets, minimizing the risk of unintentional harm. Personnel handling '''Paralysis Bullets''' are advised to wear gloves and protective equipment. Accidental skin contact with an exposed bullet can lead to unintentional paralysis, which may endanger the member and their team in hostile situations. ==Discovery== The discovery of the objects traces back to July 2016, during an exploration mission by a team of [[M.E.G]] members discovered the object on top of a table in [[Level 4]]. Initial encounters were marked by accidental contact, leading to paralysis among team members, delaying any use of the object until the effects were better understood. {{Collapse|initial='''> Open Author & Licensing Information'''|final= '''> Close Author & Licensing Information'''|body=
